items.title: Bitcoin Surged Overnight After Iran Ceasefire—This New 'AfterDark' ETF Would've Caught the Move
items.summary: Investors gained access to an ETF that’s designed to avoid the U.S. trading session, offering exposure to Bitcoin while Wall Street sleeps.

items.title: Standard Chartered mulls folding parts of Zodia Custody in-house: Bloomberg
items.summary: Standard Chartered is reportedly exploring a shake-up of Zodia Custody that would bring parts of the crypto custodian inside its own investment bank.

items.title: CZ memoir revives feud with OKX founder Star Xu over contract forgery, Huobi arrest
items.summary: CZ’s new memoir reignited his long-running feud with OKX founder Star Xu, who called the Binance founder a liar in posts on X.

items.title: Quantum threat to Bitcoin is real, but manageable, according to Wall Street broker Bernstein
items.summary: The broker said advances in quantum computing are accelerating the timeline for crypto risk, but argued Bitcoin faces a multi-year upgrade cycle, not an existential crisis.

items.title: Saylor’s solo act: JPMorgan says Strategy is practically the only thing keeping crypto flows alive
items.summary: Digital asset inflows totaled $11 billion in Q1, as institutional demand and ETF outflows left corporate buyers and venture capital as the primary sources of funding.

items.title: Instant settlement strains crypto’s capital efficiency: Ethan Buchman
items.summary: Crypto trades settle instantly, but at the cost of capital efficiency. This forces firms to overcollateralize and limits how far markets can scale, says Cosmos co-founder Ethan Buchman.

items.title: Cango sells 2,000 BTC, cuts Bitcoin production cost by 19% in March
items.summary: Bitcoin miner Cango said it sold 2,000 BTC to pay off debt and cut its BTC production cost by 19% as part of its strategic pivot to energy and AI infrastructure.

items.title: Iran eyes crypto toll for oil tanker transits through Strait of Hormuz, according to FT
items.summary: Iran plans to collect cryptocurrency and other digital payments as transit fees from fully loaded oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz, said the report.

items.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Internet Computer (ICP) rises 12.1%
items.summary: NEAR Protocol (NEAR) joined Internet Computer (ICP) as a top performer, climbing 8.9% from Tuesday.

items.title: Bitcoin’s quantum threat is distant, but migration clock is ticking, says Adam Back
items.summary: Researchers are advancing post-quantum protections even as current hardware remains far from breaking crypto systems

items.title: White House Council of Economic Advisers: Stablecoin Yield Doesn’t Threaten Small Banks
items.summary: A White House report found that banning stablecoin yield products would boost community bank lending by just 0.02%.

items.title: Morgan Stanley's bitcoin ETF opens today, giving BlackRock’s $55 billion IBIT fund its toughest rival yet
items.summary: While BlackRock’s spot bitcoin ETF currently reigns as the liquidity king of crypto, Morgan Stanley’s MSBT will leverage a market-low 0.14% fee and $7 trillion wealth management engine to possibly challenge that dominance.

items.title: South Korea tightens crypto withdrawal-delay exemptions after scam losses
items.summary: The Financial Services Commission said inconsistent exemption rules created loopholes that allowed funds to move quickly with minimal account history.

items.title: Stablecoin yields won’t harm banks, White House economists say
items.summary: White House economists say banning stablecoin yield would add little to bank lending while imposing significant costs on users.

items.title: South Korea draft bill puts stablecoins, RWAs under finance laws: Report
items.summary: The draft proposal from South Korea’s ruling party reportedly bars stablecoin interest and calls for technical standards to ensure interoperability across blockchain networks.

items.title: Thailand proposes tighter scrutiny of funders behind crypto firms
items.summary: Thailand’s SEC proposes tightening funding rules for cryptocurrency companies to include anyone backing major shareholders, directly or indirectly.
